title: galium kinuta var. viridescens
intro:
Galium kinuta var. viridescens, also known as Japanese cleavers or green bedstraw, is a variety of the Galium genus of flowering plants that is native to Japan and is characterized by its green, sticky stems and small white flowers.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Galium kinuta var. viridescens include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and even help with wound healing. In herbal preparations, it is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
